name: Power Amplifiers
description: Sub-sector template for RF power amplifier patents. Covers PA architecture (Doherty, envelope tracking, outphasing), GaN/GaAs/SiGe PA design, linearization (DPD), and PA module integration for wireless communication.
contextDescription: Power amplifiers are the most power-hungry and performance-critical component in any wireless transmitter. Every smartphone, base station, and WiFi access point contains PAs. Key vendors: Qualcomm (integrated), Skyworks, Qorvo, Broadcom (FBAR-integrated PA modules). Key innovations: envelope tracking (ET) for efficiency, Doherty for base stations, GaN for 5G infrastructure, digital pre-distortion (DPD) for linearity. PA patents are highly detectable via teardown — die architecture and matching network are visible.
scoringGuidance: Every wireless transmitter needs a PA — smartphones have 5-10 PA modules each.
Envelope tracking (ET) patents are critical — ET is now standard in flagship smartphones.
Doherty PA patents dominate base station market — every macro cell uses Doherty PAs.
GaN PA patents are high-value for 5G massive MIMO base stations.
DPD (digital pre-distortion) patents are implemented in every base station PA.

questions.question: What PA architecture does this patent address? Score 8-10 for envelope tracking, Doherty, or outphasing architectures with novel improvements, 5-7 for class-AB/class-E/class-F with efficiency innovations, 3-4 for general PA design, 1-2 for no specific architecture.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Envelope tracking and Doherty are the two dominant PA architectures — identify which.

questions.question: What semiconductor technology does this PA use? Score 8-10 for GaN-on-SiC (5G infrastructure) or advanced SiGe/SOI integration, 5-7 for GaAs HBT (smartphone) or GaN-on-Si, 3-4 for general CMOS PA, 1-2 for technology-agnostic.
questions.reasoningPrompt: GaN for infrastructure, GaAs for handset — identify the target market from the technology.

questions.question: Does this patent address PA linearization (DPD, predistortion, feedback linearization)? Score 8-10 for novel DPD algorithms or wideband linearization, 5-7 for standard DPD improvements, 3-4 for basic linearization, 1-2 for no linearization aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: DPD is mandatory in every base station PA — widely implemented.

questions.question: Does this patent improve PA efficiency (back-off efficiency, peak efficiency, average efficiency)? Score 8-10 for >50% PAE at back-off or novel efficiency enhancement technique, 5-7 for meaningful efficiency improvement, 3-4 for general efficiency awareness, 1-2 for no efficiency aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: PA efficiency directly affects battery life (handset) and operating cost (base station).

questions.question: Does this patent address PA module integration (PA + filter + switch, front-end module)? Score 8-10 for novel PAMiD (PA module with integrated duplexer) or multi-band PA module, 5-7 for PA + matching network integration, 3-4 for PA die with output matching, 1-2 for standalone PA die.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Module integration is the trend — fewer discrete components, more integrated FEM.

questions.question: How detectable is this PA innovation via chip/module teardown? Score 8-10 for die-level architecture visible in SEM (matching networks, topology), 5-7 for circuit techniques detectable via probing, 3-4 for firmware/calibration, 1-2 for conceptual only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: PA die and module architectures are routinely analyzed in TechInsights teardowns.
